paragraph: Meridian Equity Partners and co-investor Westbrook Institutional Fund completed the $178 million acquisition of the Fairlawn Promenade, a 495,000-square-foot open-air lifestyle center in the affluent Fairlawn submarket of Greenvale. The seller, Corning Retail Ventures, had owned the center since its development in 2005. Fairlawn Promenade is anchored by a 62,000-square-foot specialty grocer and a 35,000-square-foot health and wellness operator, with 65 additional tenants including a curated mix of regional and national specialty retailers, restaurants, and personal services providers. Occupancy at closing was 95.8 percent, and inline sales productivity averaged $540 per square foot for the trailing 12 months ended December 2025, well above the national average for comparable lifestyle centers. In-place net operating income is approximately $10.4 million, reflecting a going-in cap rate of 5.84 percent. Financing consisted of a $112 million ten-year fixed-rate loan from Pemberton Life at 5.42 percent. Meridian and Westbrook plan a $14.5 million refresh of the center's common areas, landscaping, signage, and seasonal programming infrastructure over the first 24 months of ownership. Eastgate Retail Advisors exclusively represented the seller.
restate_0: Meridian Equity Partners and Westbrook Institutional Fund paid $178 million for the Fairlawn Promenade, a 495,000-square-foot open-air lifestyle center in Greenvale's affluent Fairlawn submarket. Corning Retail Ventures, which developed the center in 2005 and has owned it since, was the seller. The 95.8-percent-occupied center is anchored by a 62,000-square-foot specialty grocer and a 35,000-square-foot health and wellness operator and includes 65 additional tenants. Inline sales productivity averaged $540 per square foot in the 12 months ended December 2025. In-place NOI of approximately $10.4 million implies a 5.84 percent going-in cap rate. Pemberton Life provided a $112 million ten-year fixed-rate loan at 5.42 percent. The buyers plan a $14.5 million common area refresh over the first 24 months. Eastgate Retail Advisors represented the seller.
restate_1: Meridian Equity Partners and Westbrook Institutional Fund are pleased to announce the acquisition of Fairlawn Promenade — one of Greenvale's most productive and well-regarded lifestyle retail destinations — for $178 million. This 495,000-square-foot open-air center, developed by Corning Retail Ventures in 2005, represents the best of what lifestyle retail can be: a 95.8-percent-occupied collection of 67 tenants anchored by a specialty grocer and a health and wellness operator, delivering $540 per square foot in inline sales productivity that is well above national benchmarks. In-place NOI of approximately $10.4 million at a 5.84 percent going-in cap rate provides a compelling current income foundation. Pemberton Life's $112 million fixed-rate loan at 5.42 percent reflects institutional confidence in this exceptional retail asset. Our $14.5 million refresh of common areas, landscaping, signage, and seasonal programming will ensure Fairlawn Promenade continues to deliver the curated, experiential environment that the Fairlawn community expects.
restate_2: Meridian Equity Partners and Westbrook Institutional Fund's $178 million acquisition of Fairlawn Promenade at a 5.84 percent going-in cap rate warrants honest assessment of the lifestyle retail segment's specific risks. The $540 per square foot in inline sales productivity is a genuinely strong metric and well above comparable lifestyle center averages, which validates the center's position in the Fairlawn affluent consumer market. However, a 2005-development open-air lifestyle center faces a competitive environment that has shifted significantly: the format's success depends on continuously refreshing the tenant mix as brands evolve, requiring active landlord curation and capital allocation for tenant improvement allowances not captured in the base NOI. The $14.5 million common area refresh over 24 months is an appropriate reinvestment but represents only $29.29 per square foot — modest for a 21-year-old center targeting continued premium positioning. Pemberton Life's $112 million loan at 5.42 percent is modestly below the 5.84 percent going-in cap rate, creating positive leverage, but the spread is thin and depends on maintaining NOI through the refresh cycle. The 65-tenant, two-anchor structure provides diversification but also management complexity.
restate_3: Meridian Equity Partners and Westbrook Institutional Fund's acquisition of Fairlawn Promenade at 5.84 percent demonstrates the continued pricing premium that well-curated, high-performing open-air lifestyle retail commands from institutional capital. The center's $540 per square foot in inline sales productivity — well above the national comparable average — reflects the spending power of the Fairlawn affluent consumer catchment and the effectiveness of the specialty grocer and health and wellness anchor combination in driving center traffic. Corning Retail Ventures' 21-year ownership since the 2005 development delivered an asset at the top of its competitive cycle, with 95.8 percent occupancy and a 67-tenant roster providing income stability. Pemberton Life's $112 million fixed-rate loan at 5.42 percent is competitively priced for lifestyle retail collateral with demonstrated sales productivity. The $14.5 million common area investment by Meridian and Westbrook over the first 24 months is both a value-enhancing initiative and an ownership transition signal to tenants — a standard first-year investment for acquirers of matured lifestyle centers. Eastgate Retail Advisors' exclusive representation generated competitive buyer interest.
restate_4: The Fairlawn Promenade acquisition by Meridian Equity Partners and Westbrook Institutional Fund captured the attention of retail investors tracking high-productivity lifestyle center transactions in 2026. Corning Retail Ventures' decision to exit after 21 years of development ownership of the 495,000-square-foot open-air center was well-timed: the $540 per square foot in inline sales productivity and 95.8 percent occupancy created a compelling disposition narrative in a retail investment market where comparable performance metrics were increasingly scarce. The $178 million sale at a 5.84 percent cap rate validated the flight-to-quality dynamic in lifestyle retail: premium centers in affluent submarkets continued to trade at sub-6 percent caps while weaker product languished at elevated vacancy levels. Meridian and Westbrook's $14.5 million common area refresh over the first 24 months was completed on schedule and positively received by tenants and shoppers alike. Pemberton Life's $112 million fixed-rate loan at 5.42 percent remained a competitive financing instrument through the hold period, and the center's in-place NOI grew from $10.4 million to $11.6 million by the third year of ownership.

paragraph: Thornbury Industrial Fund II, a closed-end value-add fund managed by Thornbury Capital Group, secured a $41 million senior mortgage refinancing of the Elmwood Distribution Hub, a 298,000-square-foot multi-tenant industrial park in the Elmwood submarket of Portcliffe. The loan, originated by Duncastle Community Savings at a fixed rate of 5.68 percent over seven years on a 25-year amortization schedule, replaces a $29 million floating-rate construction loan from 2022. The park was developed by Thornbury and completed in phases through early 2024, with full lease-up achieved in September 2025. Current occupancy is 100 percent across nine tenants, with in-place rents averaging $10.20 per square foot and a weighted average remaining lease term of 5.6 years. Based on a stabilized net operating income of $2.85 million and a third-party appraised value of $72.5 million, the new loan represents a 56.6 percent loan-to-value ratio. The refinancing generates $9.8 million in cash-out proceeds for Thornbury Industrial Fund II, to be redeployed into a planned speculative 185,000-square-foot industrial facility on an adjacent two-acre land parcel acquired in 2023. Whitmore Commercial Mortgage arranged the refinancing.
restate_0: Thornbury Industrial Fund II refinanced the Elmwood Distribution Hub, a fully leased 298,000-square-foot multi-tenant industrial park in Portcliffe's Elmwood submarket, with a $41 million seven-year fixed-rate mortgage from Duncastle Community Savings at 5.68 percent on a 25-year amortization schedule. The loan replaces a $29 million floating-rate construction loan from 2022. The nine-tenant park, completed through early 2024 and fully leased since September 2025, generates $2.85 million in stabilized NOI at $10.20 per square foot average in-place rents and a 5.6-year weighted average remaining lease term. A third-party appraisal of $72.5 million supports a 56.6 percent LTV. The transaction generates $9.8 million in cash-out proceeds, which Thornbury will deploy toward a speculative 185,000-square-foot industrial facility on an adjacent two-acre parcel. Whitmore Commercial Mortgage arranged the refinancing.
restate_1: Thornbury Capital Group announces the successful refinancing of the Elmwood Distribution Hub — a milestone that represents the maturation of Thornbury Industrial Fund II's development program in the Portcliffe Elmwood submarket. The $41 million seven-year fixed-rate loan from Duncastle Community Savings at 5.68 percent replaces the floating-rate construction financing from 2022 with a stable, long-term instrument more appropriate for a fully stabilized park. The Elmwood Distribution Hub achieved 100 percent occupancy in September 2025, with nine tenants occupying 298,000 square feet at $10.20 per square foot and a 5.6-year weighted average remaining lease term — metrics that supported the $72.5 million third-party appraisal and the 56.6 percent loan-to-value. The $9.8 million in cash-out proceeds will immediately be deployed toward our next speculative development — a 185,000-square-foot industrial facility on the adjacent two-acre parcel we acquired in 2023. Whitmore Commercial Mortgage's expertise in arranging this refinancing was instrumental to achieving our capital recycling objectives.
restate_2: Thornbury Industrial Fund II's $41 million refinancing of the Elmwood Distribution Hub is a clean execution of the stabilize-and-recycle industrial development strategy, with one element worth examining: the $9.8 million cash-out proceeds being deployed into a speculative 185,000-square-foot industrial facility on an adjacent two-acre parcel. Adding speculative development risk to a fully stabilized asset refinancing is a deliberate capital recycling decision, but investors in Thornbury Industrial Fund II should understand that the adjacent development — presumably from a closed-end fund that may be approaching its investment period — introduces a new risk layer as cash-out proceeds are redeployed rather than returned. The $41 million loan at 5.68 percent fixed for seven years against a $72.5 million appraisal is conservatively structured at 56.6 percent LTV, providing adequate debt service coverage against $2.85 million of stabilized NOI. The replacement of the 2022 floating-rate construction loan with fixed-rate permanent debt eliminates rate risk on the refinanced collateral. The nine-tenant, 100 percent occupied park with $10.20 per square foot average rents and 5.6-year WALT is a strong stabilization outcome for a phased development completed through early 2024.
restate_3: Thornbury Capital Group's Elmwood Distribution Hub refinancing is a well-executed stabilized-asset capital event that demonstrates the value-add industrial development model in a secondary market like Portcliffe. The park was built in phases, completed through early 2024, and fully leased by September 2025 — a 20-month absorption period for a nine-tenant, 298,000-square-foot multi-tenant park that reflects healthy Elmwood submarket demand. At $10.20 per square foot average rents and 100 percent occupancy, the park's $2.85 million stabilized NOI supported a $72.5 million third-party appraisal — a significant premium above development cost that validated Thornbury's speculative construction decision. Duncastle Community Savings' $41 million fixed-rate mortgage at 5.68 percent over seven years on a 25-year amortization schedule provides a stable, long-term financing platform. The $9.8 million cash-out proceeds earmarked for the adjacent two-acre speculative development demonstrate how stabilized industrial refinancings function as self-funding capital recycling mechanisms within closed-end development funds. Whitmore Commercial Mortgage's arrangement expertise was central to the execution.
restate_4: The Elmwood Distribution Hub refinancing completed Thornbury Industrial Fund II's value-creation arc for this Portcliffe submarket development: from floating-rate construction financing in 2022, through phased delivery and full lease-up by September 2025, to stabilized permanent debt at a 56.6 percent LTV and $9.8 million in cash-out for the next development phase. Duncastle Community Savings' willingness to provide $41 million at 5.68 percent fixed for seven years against a $72.5 million appraised value reflected the lender's comfort with the nine-tenant, 100 percent leased collateral at $10.20 per square foot and 5.6 years of weighted average remaining lease term. The $9.8 million cash-out proceeds were deployed into the adjacent two-acre speculative industrial facility as planned, with the 185,000-square-foot building breaking ground in early 2026. That speculative facility was 70 percent pre-leased before completion — a signal of continued Elmwood submarket demand strength and Thornbury's execution reputation in the local industrial market. The Elmwood Distribution Hub's stabilized performance remained above expectations through the loan term.

paragraph: Elmore Realty Trust, a publicly traded net-lease REIT focused on essential retail, announced the completion of its disposition of 35 properties for total proceeds of $187.3 million, reflecting a blended exit cap rate of 5.9 percent. The assets, comprising 22 quick-service restaurant properties, nine automotive service centers, and four convenience stores with fuel, were sold across three separate portfolio transactions to three different buyers over a 60-day marketing period. Weighted average remaining lease term across the divested portfolio was 7.4 years. Net proceeds, after satisfaction of $102 million in property-level mortgage debt, total approximately $85 million. Elmore Realty Trust will reinvest proceeds into higher-growth metropolitan markets with stronger rent growth profiles and longer lease durations. Following the dispositions, Elmore's portfolio totals 1,108 properties in 36 states, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 9.8 years and an average going-in cap rate on recent acquisitions of 5.65 percent. The company reaffirmed full-year 2026 adjusted funds from operations guidance of $1.84 to $1.90 per diluted share. Redfern Net Lease Advisors ran the marketing process for all three portfolio transactions.
restate_0: Elmore Realty Trust sold 35 net-lease properties — 22 quick-service restaurant locations, nine automotive service centers, and four convenience stores with fuel — across three separate portfolio transactions for total proceeds of $187.3 million at a blended exit cap rate of 5.9 percent. The 60-day marketing process, managed by Redfern Net Lease Advisors, produced three separate buyers. The divested portfolio had a weighted average remaining lease term of 7.4 years. Net proceeds after retiring $102 million in property-level mortgage debt total approximately $85 million, which Elmore plans to reinvest in higher-growth metropolitan markets with stronger rent growth and longer lease terms. Elmore's portfolio now totals 1,108 properties in 36 states with a 9.8-year weighted average remaining lease term. The company reaffirmed 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.84 to $1.90 per diluted share.
restate_1: Elmore Realty Trust announces the successful completion of its strategic disposition of 35 properties for total proceeds of $187.3 million — a portfolio pruning initiative that demonstrates both the liquidity of our net-lease portfolio and the discipline of our capital allocation strategy. The three-buyer, three-portfolio structure executed over 60 days through Redfern Net Lease Advisors generated a blended 5.9 percent exit cap rate, monetizing assets whose shorter remaining lease terms and market profiles were better suited to other ownership structures. The $85 million in net proceeds, after retiring $102 million in property-level debt, will be redeployed into higher-growth metropolitan market acquisitions offering stronger rent growth profiles and longer lease durations — consistent with our stated portfolio enhancement priorities. Post-disposition, our 1,108-property, 36-state portfolio carries a 9.8-year weighted average remaining lease term, improved from pre-disposition levels. We reaffirm our 2026 adjusted FFO guidance of $1.84 to $1.90 per diluted share.
restate_2: Elmore Realty Trust's 35-property disposition program at a 5.9 percent blended exit cap rate raises several questions about the portfolio rotation strategy. Selling quick-service restaurant, automotive service, and convenience store assets at 5.9 percent — a category where the bid for short-duration net-lease assets is typically thinner — suggests either competitive buyer interest or a pricing concession to achieve the 60-day execution timeline. The weighted average remaining lease term of 7.4 years on the divested assets is actually reasonable for net-lease product, not short enough to typically justify a discount sale. The $102 million in property-level mortgage debt retired from $187.3 million in total proceeds means only $85 million in net cash is available for redeployment — a smaller capital base for the stated redeployment into higher-growth metropolitan markets. At a blended exit cap rate of 5.9 percent versus a going-in acquisition cap rate of 5.65 percent on recent purchases, Elmore is selling at a wider cap rate than it is buying — a spread that could generate meaningful short-term income dilution during the redeployment period. The 2026 AFFO guidance reaffirmation at $1.84 to $1.90 per diluted share needs to be evaluated against the dilution from this spread.
restate_3: Elmore Realty Trust's 35-property portfolio disposition at a 5.9 percent blended exit cap rate is a portfolio quality improvement strategy executed through three discrete market transactions — an efficient structure that avoided single large-portfolio discounts while achieving the 60-day target timeline. Redfern Net Lease Advisors' marketing approach of segmenting the 35 properties into three sub-portfolios by asset type — quick-service restaurant, automotive, and convenience store — allowed each buyer to acquire within their specific product competency, generating competitive pricing within each category. The blended 5.9 percent exit cap rate reflects the market's pricing of 7.4-year WALT net-lease assets with essential retail tenancy profiles. The $85 million in net proceeds available for redeployment is less than the gross proceeds suggest, but the concurrent debt retirement improves Elmore's unencumbered asset pool — an often overlooked balance sheet benefit. The 1,108-property, 36-state portfolio with a 9.8-year weighted average remaining lease term positions Elmore for AFFO growth above the $1.84 to $1.90 per diluted share 2026 guidance.
restate_4: Elmore Realty Trust's 35-property disposition program was recognized by the net-lease REIT investor community as a strategic pruning initiative that improved portfolio quality metrics at a reasonable cost. The three-portfolio structure managed by Redfern Net Lease Advisors — matching 22 quick-service restaurant properties, nine automotive service centers, and four convenience stores with fuel to three distinct buyers over 60 days — demonstrated the depth of institutional buyer demand for net-lease product in the 7.4-year WALT range. The blended 5.9 percent exit cap rate compared favorably to prevailing secondary market cap rates for comparable assets, suggesting Elmore extracted near-market pricing on properties it had identified as below-target quality. The $85 million in net proceeds after retiring $102 million in property-level debt was redeployed over the subsequent two quarters into industrial-adjacent essential retail acquisitions in higher-growth metropolitan markets, with average going-in cap rates of 5.65 percent and weighted average lease terms exceeding 11 years. The 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.84 to $1.90 per diluted share was achieved at the midpoint, with the redeployment completing before year-end contributed to the upper end of the range.

paragraph: Crestview Residential Partners announced the $67.3 million preferred equity investment by Argent Institutional Partners in the Crestview Summit, a 482-unit luxury multifamily high-rise under construction at 2400 Summit Drive in the Ridgecrest neighborhood of Portsmith. The preferred equity carries an 8.5 percent current pay return plus a 1.5 percent accrual, with a projected three-year term aligned to the anticipated stabilization and refinancing of the development. Total project cost is $238 million, funded through a combination of a $148 million construction loan from Riverton Commercial Bank at SOFR plus 175 basis points, the $67.3 million in preferred equity, and $22.7 million in common equity contributed by Crestview Residential Partners. The 42-story tower is scheduled to deliver its first units in May 2027 and achieve 95-percent occupancy by late 2027. Projected market rents range from $2,400 for studios to $5,800 for three-bedroom penthouse units, with an average effective rent of $3,050 per unit. The project includes ground-floor retail totaling 4,800 square feet and below-grade parking for 420 vehicles. Portsmith's Ridgecrest submarket absorbed 1,640 new apartment units in 2025, with market occupancy remaining at 95.1 percent. Pelham Structured Capital advised Crestview on the preferred equity placement.
restate_0: Argent Institutional Partners committed $67.3 million in preferred equity to the Crestview Summit, a 482-unit, 42-story luxury multifamily tower under construction at 2400 Summit Drive in Portsmith's Ridgecrest neighborhood. The preferred equity carries an 8.5 percent current pay return and a 1.5 percent accrual over a projected three-year term. The $238 million total project is also financed by a $148 million construction loan from Riverton Commercial Bank at SOFR plus 175 basis points and $22.7 million in common equity from Crestview Residential Partners. First units are expected in May 2027, with 95 percent occupancy targeted by late 2027. Projected rents range from $2,400 for studios to $5,800 for three-bedroom penthouses, with an average of $3,050 per unit. The project includes 4,800 square feet of retail and 420 below-grade parking spaces. Ridgecrest absorbed 1,640 units in 2025 with market occupancy at 95.1 percent. Pelham Structured Capital advised Crestview.
restate_1: Crestview Residential Partners is excited to announce the $67.3 million preferred equity commitment from Argent Institutional Partners for the Crestview Summit — a transformative 482-unit luxury high-rise that will reshape the Portsmith skyline and redefine the Ridgecrest neighborhood's residential market. Argent's investment, carrying an 8.5 percent current pay return plus 1.5 percent accrual, reflects the institutional conviction in both the Crestview Summit development and the Ridgecrest submarket's absorption capacity. The Portsmith Ridgecrest market's 2025 performance speaks for itself: 1,640 new apartment units absorbed with market occupancy holding at 95.1 percent. Our 42-story tower will deliver 482 luxury units ranging from $2,400 studios to $5,800 three-bedroom penthouses, with an average effective rent of $3,050, beginning in May 2027. Total project investment of $238 million — including $148 million in Riverton Commercial Bank construction financing — reflects our commitment to delivering the highest-quality residential product in Portsmith. Pelham Structured Capital's advisory expertise was instrumental in this preferred equity placement.
restate_2: The Crestview Summit preferred equity structure deserves careful analysis from investors assessing the capital stack. The $67.3 million preferred equity at 8.5 percent current pay plus 1.5 percent accrual sits between $148 million of senior construction debt and $22.7 million of common equity in a $238 million total project. The common equity cushion of $22.7 million — 9.5 percent of total project cost — is thin relative to the risk profile of a 42-story, 482-unit luxury tower with a May 2027 first-unit delivery target and late 2027 stabilization goal. At a $3,050 average effective rent across 482 units, the project's stabilized gross residential revenue before vacancy and expenses is approximately $17.6 million annually — supporting a total capitalization of $238 million requires sustained occupancy and rent performance through a competitive Portsmith luxury delivery cycle. The preferred equity's 10 percent blended return (8.5 current plus 1.5 accrual) creates a substantial hurdle above which the common equity's $22.7 million must be recovered before preferred investors earn their return. Argent Institutional Partners' three-year term aligned to stabilization and refinancing is appropriate but depends on Portsmith luxury demand remaining near the 95.1 percent market occupancy level through the absorption period.
restate_3: Argent Institutional Partners' $67.3 million preferred equity commitment to the Crestview Summit is a structurally nuanced investment that bridges the gap between Riverton Commercial Bank's senior construction debt and Crestview Residential Partners' common equity — a capital stack configuration that has become increasingly common for luxury multifamily development financing in the current market. The 8.5 percent current pay plus 1.5 percent accrual at 10 percent blended return is competitive for three-year preferred equity in a luxury lease-up development in a market with 95.1 percent occupancy. Portsmith's Ridgecrest submarket absorbed 1,640 units in 2025 while maintaining that occupancy level, suggesting above-average demand depth that supports the late 2027 95 percent stabilization target. The $3,050 average effective rent across studio-to-penthouse units in a 42-story luxury tower positions Crestview Summit in the top tier of the Portsmith market. Pelham Structured Capital's advisory role demonstrates the increasing specialization in preferred equity placement for large-scale luxury multifamily developments. Crestview Residential Partners' $22.7 million common equity represents their development fee and promoted interest exposure.
restate_4: The Crestview Summit preferred equity placement by Argent Institutional Partners was a 2026 transaction that reflected the maturation of structured capital markets for luxury multifamily development in secondary metropolitan markets like Portsmith. Crestview Residential Partners' ability to secure $67.3 million in preferred equity at a 10 percent blended return (8.5 percent current pay plus 1.5 percent accrual) alongside a $148 million construction loan from Riverton Commercial Bank on a $238 million 42-story tower project demonstrated strong institutional confidence in the development and the Ridgecrest market. The late 2027 stabilization target was achieved — 95 percent occupancy was reached in October 2027, with average effective rents of $3,110 slightly above the $3,050 projection. The preferred equity's three-year term aligned precisely with the refinancing into permanent debt in mid-2028, generating the expected return for Argent Institutional Partners and distributing residual equity to Crestview Residential Partners. Portsmith's Ridgecrest submarket continued to absorb luxury product at sub-5 percent vacancy through 2028, validating the market selection.

paragraph: Dunmore Capital Trust announced the $249 million refinancing of the Dunmore Tower, a 45-story, 960,000-square-foot Class A office skyscraper in the Central Business District of Calderton. The new financing, originated by a three-lender syndicate comprising Aldwick National Bank, Riverton Life Insurance, and Greyfield Mortgage Trust, replaces $198 million in existing CMBS debt that was approaching its final maturity date. The loan carries a fixed all-in rate of 5.74 percent over a seven-year term with a 30-year amortization schedule commencing after three years of interest-only payments. Loan proceeds include $44 million in cash-out, net of transaction costs. The tower is 91 percent leased to 18 tenants, with a weighted average remaining lease term of 6.2 years and effective gross annual income of approximately $48 million. The refinancing was based on an independent appraisal of $430 million, implying a loan-to-value ratio of 57.9 percent. Since acquiring the tower in 2014 for $312 million, Dunmore Capital Trust has invested $38 million in capital improvements including elevator modernization, lobby renovation, and the addition of a tenant conference center and fitness facility. Westbridge Commercial Finance arranged the refinancing.
restate_0: Dunmore Capital Trust refinanced the Dunmore Tower, a 45-story, 960,000-square-foot Class A office skyscraper in Calderton's CBD, with a $249 million fixed-rate loan at 5.74 percent from a syndicate of Aldwick National Bank, Riverton Life Insurance, and Greyfield Mortgage Trust. The loan replaces $198 million in maturing CMBS debt and carries a seven-year term with three years of interest-only payments followed by 30-year amortization. Net cash-out proceeds total $44 million after transaction costs. The 91-percent-leased tower generates approximately $48 million in effective gross annual income from 18 tenants with a 6.2-year weighted average remaining lease term. A $430 million independent appraisal supports a 57.9 percent LTV. Dunmore has owned the tower since 2014, investing $38 million in improvements. Westbridge Commercial Finance arranged the refinancing.
restate_1: Dunmore Capital Trust is pleased to announce the successful refinancing of the Dunmore Tower — the crown jewel of Calderton's Central Business District — with a $249 million fixed-rate financing from a three-lender syndicate that provides long-term capital certainty for one of the city's most iconic office buildings. The new loan's 5.74 percent fixed rate for seven years, originated by Aldwick National Bank, Riverton Life Insurance, and Greyfield Mortgage Trust, replaces CMBS debt that had been approaching its final maturity. Our conservative 57.9 percent loan-to-value ratio, based on a $430 million independent appraisal, reflects both the strength of our asset and the discipline of our capital structure management. Since our 2014 acquisition at $312 million, we have invested $38 million in elevator modernization, lobby renovation, and tenant amenity additions — investments that contributed to the 91 percent occupancy and $48 million effective gross income that underpin this financing. The $44 million in cash-out proceeds will be deployed into additional portfolio initiatives. Westbridge Commercial Finance's expertise was critical to this syndicated execution.
restate_2: Dunmore Capital Trust's $249 million refinancing of the Dunmore Tower requires scrutiny of both the CMBS maturity management and the underlying office fundamentals. The trust was refinancing $198 million in CMBS debt approaching its final maturity — a common deadline-driven transaction that may have limited the negotiating flexibility for optimal loan terms. The 5.74 percent fixed all-in rate for seven years on a 960,000-square-foot CBD office tower is reasonable but not exceptional for institutional office collateral in the current market. The $430 million appraisal against a 2014 acquisition cost of $312 million plus $38 million in improvements implies a total cost basis of $350 million — suggesting the tower has appreciated modestly over 12 years, less than many CBD office towers in markets not affected by structural vacancy trends. At 91 percent occupancy with 6.2 years of WALT and $48 million in effective gross annual income, the tower's fundamentals are solid but not extraordinary. The $44 million cash-out is the most debatable element: using refinancing proceeds for unspecified portfolio initiatives rather than reducing leverage may concern investors monitoring Dunmore Capital Trust's balance sheet in an environment of elevated office sector risk.
restate_3: Dunmore Capital Trust's Dunmore Tower refinancing illustrates the continuing institutional lender appetite for well-leased, seasoned CBD office collateral from established sponsors in established markets. The three-lender syndicate — Aldwick National Bank, Riverton Life Insurance, and Greyfield Mortgage Trust — bringing different institutional perspectives to a $249 million office refinancing is a constructive signal for the senior debt market. At 57.9 percent LTV on a $430 million appraisal, the loan is conservatively sized relative to the collateral value, providing meaningful lender protection even under a market-value stress scenario. The 91 percent occupancy across 18 tenants with 6.2 years of weighted average remaining lease term is above the industry average for major CBD office towers and reflects Dunmore Capital Trust's active leasing and capital investment program since 2014. The $38 million invested in elevator modernization, lobby renovation, and tenant amenities has clearly contributed to maintaining tenant retention in a competitive market. The three-year interest-only period aligns with Dunmore's anticipated asset management actions over the near term. Westbridge Commercial Finance's arrangement of the syndicate was essential to the execution.
restate_4: The Dunmore Tower refinancing was a material balance sheet event for Dunmore Capital Trust and one of the larger CBD office refinancings completed in Calderton during 2026. The replacement of $198 million in approaching-maturity CMBS debt with a $249 million fixed-rate syndicated loan at 5.74 percent from Aldwick National Bank, Riverton Life Insurance, and Greyfield Mortgage Trust accomplished three objectives: eliminated the CMBS maturity cliff, generated $44 million in net cash-out proceeds, and extended the debt maturity horizon by seven years. The Dunmore Tower's 91 percent occupancy and $48 million in effective gross annual income from 18 tenants with a 6.2-year weighted average remaining lease term provided the underwriting comfort that the three-lender syndicate required. The $430 million appraisal was supported by a consistent stream of capital investment since the 2014 acquisition — $38 million in elevator, lobby, and amenity improvements that maintained the building's competitive position in the Calderton CBD. The $44 million in cash-out proceeds were subsequently deployed into industrial acquisitions consistent with Dunmore's broader portfolio strategy.

paragraph: Lakemont Property Partners completed the sale of the Lakemont Corporate Center, a 16-acre, five-building suburban office campus totaling 437,000 square feet in the Lakemont Business Park submarket of Grenville, for $38.5 million, or $88.10 per square foot. The buyer, Meridian Adaptive Realty Partners, plans to convert the campus to a healthcare and medical office use through a phased redevelopment. At sale, the campus was 52 percent occupied, with in-place rents of $19.50 per square foot, full service. The going-in cap rate on occupied income was 5.2 percent, with an all-in acquisition cap rate of approximately 2.7 percent reflecting the vacancy drag. Meridian intends to invest $58 million over three years to upgrade mechanical systems, add surgery suites, increase parking, and subdivide floor plates to accommodate medical tenants. Target stabilized rent upon redevelopment completion is $28 per square foot on a full-service basis across the converted buildings, with projected stabilized net operating income of $5.4 million. Meridian financed the acquisition and redevelopment program with a $62 million construction and renovation loan from Portside Commercial Lending. Cornhill Property Advisors and Halcrest Realty Services co-brokered the transaction.
restate_0: Meridian Adaptive Realty Partners acquired the Lakemont Corporate Center, a 437,000-square-foot, five-building suburban office campus in Grenville's Lakemont Business Park submarket, from Lakemont Property Partners for $38.5 million, or $88.10 per square foot, with plans for conversion to healthcare and medical office use. At sale, the 52-percent-occupied campus generated in-place rents of $19.50 per square foot full-service, with a 5.2 percent cap rate on occupied income and approximately 2.7 percent on an all-in basis. Meridian plans a $58 million, three-year redevelopment adding surgery suites, upgraded mechanical systems, increased parking, and subdivided floor plates. Target stabilized rents are $28 per square foot with projected stabilized NOI of $5.4 million. Portside Commercial Lending provided a $62 million construction and renovation loan. Cornhill Property Advisors and Halcrest Realty Services co-brokered the transaction.
restate_1: Meridian Adaptive Realty Partners is pleased to announce the acquisition of the Lakemont Corporate Center in Grenville's Lakemont Business Park — a transformative opportunity to reimagine a 437,000-square-foot, five-building suburban campus as a premier healthcare and medical office destination. The Grenville market's growing healthcare demand, driven by an aging population and expanding health system footprint in the region, creates the tenant base that will ultimately fill this redeveloped campus at our target stabilized rent of $28 per square foot. Our $58 million, three-year redevelopment program — adding surgery suites, upgrading mechanical systems, increasing parking, and subdividing floor plates for medical tenant requirements — will comprehensively transform each building for its healthcare future. Portside Commercial Lending's $62 million construction and renovation loan provides the execution capital to complete this vision. We thank Lakemont Property Partners for the opportunity and Cornhill Property Advisors and Halcrest Realty Services for their professional brokerage of this transaction.
restate_2: Meridian Adaptive Realty Partners' $38.5 million acquisition of the Lakemont Corporate Center — a 52-percent-occupied, 437,000-square-foot suburban office campus in Grenville — is a high-risk conversion play that requires careful evaluation of both the healthcare tenant demand thesis and the redevelopment economics. The all-in acquisition cap rate of approximately 2.7 percent, reflecting the 48 percent vacancy, means Meridian is paying primarily for conversion optionality rather than current income. Adding $58 million in redevelopment costs to the $38.5 million acquisition price produces a total investment of $96.5 million against a projected stabilized NOI of $5.4 million — an implied yield on total investment of approximately 5.6 percent, which is tight for a suburban medical office conversion carrying significant execution risk. Surgery suite additions require regulatory approvals, specialized mechanical infrastructure, and certified contractor access that routinely extend timelines and inflate budgets. The $62 million construction and renovation loan from Portside Commercial Lending covers the combined acquisition and renovation cost but introduces leverage against unstabilized collateral. The $28 per square foot target stabilized full-service rent is achievable for medical office but requires sustained healthcare tenant demand in the Grenville submarket.
restate_3: Meridian Adaptive Realty Partners' acquisition and planned conversion of the Lakemont Corporate Center illustrates the medical office conversion thesis at its most capital-intensive: a 52-percent-occupied suburban office campus acquired below $90 per square foot and redeveloped through a $58 million program to serve an entirely different demand segment. Healthcare is one of the most credible redevelopment demand anchors for underperforming suburban office: physician groups, outpatient surgery centers, and health system ancillary services require suburban floor plates that cannot find campus-adjacent space near oversubscribed medical buildings, and they bring long-duration, credit-backed tenancy. The $28 per square foot stabilized rent target — versus $19.50 per square foot in-place conventional office rent at 52 percent occupancy — reflects the medical premium that purpose-converted space commands. At $5.4 million of projected stabilized NOI on a total investment of approximately $96.5 million, the exit yield on total cost is approximately 5.6 percent. Cornhill Property Advisors and Halcrest Realty Services co-brokered a transaction that reflects the market's bifurcated assessment of suburban office versus adaptive reuse value.
restate_4: The Lakemont Corporate Center transaction between Lakemont Property Partners and Meridian Adaptive Realty Partners captured the attention of the Grenville office and healthcare real estate markets. Lakemont's exit at $38.5 million — $88.10 per square foot on a 52-percent-occupied campus — was a realistic pricing outcome for a distressed suburban office asset in 2026 and allowed the seller to avoid the carrying costs of a vacant-trajectory building. Meridian's $58 million conversion investment, combined with the $38.5 million acquisition, represented a total $96.5 million program against a $5.4 million projected stabilized NOI — a 5.6 percent return on total investment that required successful medical leasing at $28 per square foot to achieve. The three-year conversion program was completed on schedule, with the first surgery suite tenant opening in late 2027. By mid-2029, three physician groups, one outpatient surgery center, and one regional health system occupied approximately 78 percent of the converted campus. Stabilized NOI reached $4.9 million — slightly below the $5.4 million target — reflecting modest rent concessions to accelerate leasing during a competitive healthcare tenant recruitment period.

paragraph: Falconer Development Partners broke ground on the Westgate Commerce Center, a three-phase, 4.5-million-square-foot speculative industrial development on a 295-acre greenfield site in the Westgate Township submarket of Veldran. Phase I encompasses 1.2 million square feet in three separate buildings ranging from 300,000 to 480,000 square feet, all featuring 40-foot clear heights, ESFR sprinkler systems, cross-dock configurations, and 200-foot truck courts. Total Phase I development cost is projected at $98.4 million, with an anticipated yield on cost of 7.0 percent based on current market rents of $8.65 per square foot. Construction financing was arranged with Aldwick Regional Bank in the form of a $65 million floating-rate loan at SOFR plus 185 basis points over a 24-month term. The Veldran market absorbed 8.2 million square feet of industrial space in 2025, with vacancy declining to 4.7 percent. Falconer anticipates initiating lease negotiations as soon as framing is complete, targeting a combination of single-tenant buildouts and multi-tenant configurations for Phase I. Phases II and III are contingent on Phase I lease-up performance, with Phase II planned for 1.6 million square feet and Phase III for 1.7 million square feet. Cornhill Industrial Leasing is handling all Phase I marketing assignments.
restate_0: Falconer Development Partners has broken ground on the Westgate Commerce Center, a speculative three-phase industrial development totaling 4.5 million square feet on a 295-acre greenfield site in Veldran's Westgate Township submarket. Phase I covers 1.2 million square feet across three buildings of 300,000 to 480,000 square feet, featuring 40-foot clear heights, ESFR sprinkler systems, cross-dock configurations, and 200-foot truck courts. Phase I development cost is $98.4 million, targeting a 7.0 percent yield on cost at current market rents of $8.65 per square foot. Aldwick Regional Bank provided a $65 million floating-rate construction loan at SOFR plus 185 basis points over 24 months. Veldran absorbed 8.2 million square feet of industrial space in 2025 with vacancy falling to 4.7 percent. Phases II and III, at 1.6 million and 1.7 million square feet respectively, are contingent on Phase I lease-up. Cornhill Industrial Leasing is handling Phase I marketing.
restate_1: Falconer Development Partners is excited to announce the groundbreaking of the Westgate Commerce Center — our most ambitious speculative industrial development and a landmark project for the Veldran Westgate Township submarket. The three-phase, 4.5-million-square-foot campus on a 295-acre greenfield site will deliver best-in-class industrial infrastructure: 40-foot clear heights, ESFR sprinkler systems, cross-dock configurations, and 200-foot truck courts across all Phase I buildings. Phase I's 1.2 million square feet in three buildings — ranging from 300,000 to 480,000 square feet — will be marketed by Cornhill Industrial Leasing for both single-tenant and multi-tenant configurations, at projected yields on cost of 7.0 percent based on Veldran's current market rents of $8.65 per square foot. Aldwick Regional Bank's $65 million floating-rate construction loan at SOFR plus 185 basis points supports our execution. The Veldran market's 8.2 million square feet of 2025 net absorption and 4.7 percent vacancy underpin our confidence in this program. Phases II and III will follow Phase I's success.
restate_2: Falconer Development Partners' groundbreaking on the Westgate Commerce Center presents a speculative development risk profile that warrants independent analysis before Phases II and III are committed. The 4.5-million-square-foot, three-phase master plan on 295 greenfield acres in Veldran's Westgate Township submarket represents a massive bet on sustained Veldran industrial absorption beyond the 8.2 million square feet absorbed in 2025. Phase I at 1.2 million square feet with no pre-leasing is entirely speculative: the $65 million floating-rate construction loan from Aldwick Regional Bank at SOFR plus 185 basis points carries cost exposure during a 24-month term, and the 7.0 percent yield on cost at $8.65 per square foot rent assumes market rents hold through the construction and lease-up period. Speculative supply in markets that have recently absorbed significant square footage can accelerate further rent softening if multiple new projects arrive simultaneously. Phases II and III at 1.6 million and 1.7 million square feet are contingent on Phase I lease-up, which is appropriate risk management, but the total 4.5-million-square-foot program represents 55 percent of Veldran's entire 2025 net absorption — a staggering supply addition if fully delivered.
restate_3: Falconer Development Partners' Westgate Commerce Center groundbreaking is a statement of conviction in the Veldran industrial market's continued absorption capacity. The decision to break ground on 1.2 million speculative square feet — with Phases II and III contingent on Phase I lease-up — reflects a disciplined approach to large-scale industrial development that manages total exposure while preserving the ability to accelerate if demand continues. Veldran's 8.2 million square feet of 2025 net absorption and 4.7 percent vacancy are among the strongest demand metrics for any major industrial market, justifying speculative development at scale. The $98.4 million Phase I development at $8.65 per square foot market rents, targeting a 7.0 percent yield on cost, is competitive with the development economics of similar large-scale speculative programs. Aldwick Regional Bank's $65 million floating-rate loan at SOFR plus 185 basis points over 24 months is appropriate for speculative construction at this market's fundamentals. Cornhill Industrial Leasing's role as Phase I marketing agent reflects their established presence in the Veldran submarket. The 40-foot clear heights, ESFR systems, and 200-foot truck courts position these buildings for the highest-tier logistics tenants.
restate_4: The Westgate Commerce Center groundbreaking was a pivotal moment in the Veldran Westgate Township submarket's industrial development cycle. Falconer Development Partners' willingness to commit to 1.2 million speculative square feet in Phase I, on a 295-acre site capable of ultimately hosting 4.5 million square feet, sent a clear signal about developer confidence in the market's absorption trajectory. Phase I's three buildings — 300,000 to 480,000 square feet each, with 40-foot clears, ESFR, cross-dock, and 200-foot truck courts — were exactly what the Veldran market's large-scale logistics tenants were seeking. Cornhill Industrial Leasing signed the first lease on a 480,000-square-foot single-tenant building within 60 days of framing completion, validating Falconer's pre-lease-negotiations-at-framing strategy. Two additional leases for the remaining 720,000 square feet were executed before delivery. Phase I's 7.0 percent yield on cost was achieved as projected at the $8.65 per square foot market rent. Phase II's 1.6 million square feet was immediately committed based on Phase I's performance, with groundbreaking following within three months of Phase I delivery.

paragraph: Eastbrook REIT, a publicly traded diversified real estate investment trust, executed a 1031 exchange transaction, disposing of the Eastbrook Office Plaza, a 215,000-square-foot suburban office building in Alderton, for $29.8 million, and simultaneously acquiring the Thornfield Logistics Hub, a newly constructed 680,000-square-foot industrial facility in the Thornfield submarket of Veldran, for $81.4 million. The two transactions were structured to qualify as a like-kind exchange under Section 1031 of the Internal Revenue Code. The office property was sold at a cap rate of 7.4 percent and was 76 percent occupied at closing, while the industrial acquisition reflects a going-in cap rate of 5.35 percent. The Thornfield Logistics Hub is fully leased to a single tenant, Meridian Distribution Services, under a 10-year NNN lease at a rent of $8.65 per square foot with three-percent annual escalations. The balance of the industrial acquisition, $51.6 million, was funded with a new $53 million mortgage from Rivergate Life Insurance at a fixed rate of 5.48 percent. The exchange transaction was structured by Langfield 1031 Exchange Services and facilitated by Halborn Property Advisors on the industrial side and Corum Realty Services on the office disposal.
restate_0: Eastbrook REIT executed a 1031 like-kind exchange, selling the Eastbrook Office Plaza — a 215,000-square-foot suburban office building in Alderton — for $29.8 million at a 7.4 percent cap rate (76 percent occupancy) and acquiring the Thornfield Logistics Hub, a 680,000-square-foot, newly constructed industrial facility in Veldran's Thornfield submarket, for $81.4 million at a 5.35 percent cap rate. The Thornfield hub is fully leased to Meridian Distribution Services on a 10-year NNN lease at $8.65 per square foot with 3 percent annual escalations. The $51.6 million balance of the industrial acquisition beyond the exchange proceeds was funded by a new $53 million fixed-rate mortgage from Rivergate Life Insurance at 5.48 percent. Langfield 1031 Exchange Services structured the exchange; Halborn Property Advisors facilitated the industrial acquisition and Corum Realty Services handled the office disposal.
restate_1: Eastbrook REIT announces the successful execution of a 1031 like-kind exchange that simultaneously advances our portfolio repositioning strategy and optimizes our tax position. By disposing of the Eastbrook Office Plaza in Alderton — a 215,000-square-foot suburban office building where 24 percent vacancy reflects broader suburban office headwinds — at $29.8 million, and reinvesting proceeds into the Thornfield Logistics Hub in Veldran's Thornfield submarket, we have executed a capital rotation that improves our asset quality and long-term growth profile. The Thornfield Logistics Hub's 680,000 square feet are fully committed by Meridian Distribution Services on a 10-year NNN lease with 3 percent annual escalations — a stark contrast to the office exposure we are exiting. The $81.4 million acquisition at a 5.35 percent cap rate reflects the premium that newly constructed, fully leased industrial commands. Rivergate Life Insurance's $53 million fixed-rate mortgage at 5.48 percent completes the capital stack. We thank Langfield 1031 Exchange Services for structuring this complex transaction.
restate_2: Eastbrook REIT's 1031 exchange execution — selling a 215,000-square-foot suburban office building at 7.4 percent cap and acquiring a 680,000-square-foot industrial facility at 5.35 percent cap — produces a significant cap rate compression that investors should model carefully. The $29.8 million office exit generates exchange proceeds that cover only a portion of the $81.4 million industrial acquisition, requiring $51.6 million in additional funding through a $53 million Rivergate Life Insurance mortgage at 5.48 percent. The 185-basis-point gap between the office exit cap rate (7.4 percent) and the industrial acquisition cap rate (5.35 percent) represents income dilution: Eastbrook is exchanging higher-yielding income — albeit from a partially vacant office building — for lower-yielding income from a triple-net industrial lease. The long-term thesis is that the industrial asset's 3 percent annual escalations and Meridian Distribution Services' credit will compound over time to overcome the initial income dilution, but in the near term, this is an income-reducing exchange executed primarily for sector rotation and tax deferral benefits. The 1031 structure benefits Eastbrook by deferring capital gains tax on the Alderton sale, which is the primary economic rationale for the exchange structure.
restate_3: Eastbrook REIT's 1031 exchange between the Eastbrook Office Plaza and the Thornfield Logistics Hub is a prototypical portfolio repositioning tool for publicly traded REITs navigating sector allocation shifts without triggering taxable gain recognition. The simultaneous disposal of a 76-percent-occupied suburban office asset at a 7.4 percent cap rate and acquisition of a fully leased, newly constructed industrial facility at a 5.35 percent cap rate demonstrates the pricing differential between sectors in the current market — while also illustrating that REITs willing to accept short-term income dilution can accelerate sector rotation through the tax-efficient 1031 mechanism. Langfield 1031 Exchange Services' structuring role was critical to the transaction's tax qualification, ensuring the like-kind exchange complied with Section 1031 requirements. Meridian Distribution Services' 10-year NNN lease with 3 percent annual escalations provides the contractual income growth that offsets the lower initial yield relative to the sold office asset. Rivergate Life Insurance's $53 million mortgage at 5.48 percent was competitively priced for a newly constructed, fully leased industrial facility. Halborn Property Advisors and Corum Realty Services executed efficiently on their respective sides.
restate_4: The Eastbrook REIT 1031 exchange was one of 2026's cleaner examples of a publicly traded REIT using the like-kind exchange mechanism to accelerate portfolio repositioning. The disposal of the Eastbrook Office Plaza — 215,000 square feet, 76 percent occupied, 7.4 percent cap rate — at $29.8 million and the simultaneous acquisition of the Thornfield Logistics Hub — 680,000 square feet, 100 percent leased, 5.35 percent cap rate — at $81.4 million was executed with the Section 1031 structure because the office asset had a low cost basis that would otherwise have generated a significant taxable gain. Langfield 1031 Exchange Services navigated the timing and identification requirements that govern like-kind exchanges. Meridian Distribution Services' 10-year NNN lease with 3 percent annual escalations provided the contractual income stream certainty that Eastbrook's underwriting required for the industrial acquisition. Rivergate Life Insurance's $53 million fixed-rate mortgage at 5.48 percent completed the acquisition financing. In retrospect, the cap rate compression from 7.4 percent to 5.35 percent was offset by three percent annual rent growth compounding over the Meridian lease term, vindicating the exchange economics.

paragraph: Clearbrook Realty Capital completed the disposition of a 90 percent equity interest in the Clearbrook Pinnacle, a 324-unit luxury multifamily tower in the Pinnacle District of Halborn, to Sovereign Realty Income Fund, an open-end perpetual-life vehicle, for $261 million, valuing the full asset at $290 million, or approximately $895,062 per unit. Clearbrook developed the 36-story tower, which was delivered in January 2024, at a total cost of $248 million. The community achieved 95-percent occupancy within 14 months of opening and reported average effective rents of $3,620 per month for the trailing 12-month period ended March 2026. Net operating income for 2025 was $13.1 million, reflecting a stabilized cap rate of 4.52 percent on the total asset value. Sovereign Realty Income Fund financed its equity acquisition with a combination of fund equity and a $155 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Aldwick National Life at 5.62 percent. Clearbrook will retain a 10-percent interest in the joint venture and continue as property manager for a fee of 3.5 percent of effective gross income. Fernbrook Realty Advisors represented Clearbrook in the recapitalization process.
restate_0: Clearbrook Realty Capital sold a 90 percent interest in the Clearbrook Pinnacle, a 36-story, 324-unit luxury multifamily tower in Halborn's Pinnacle District, to Sovereign Realty Income Fund for $261 million, valuing the full asset at $290 million, or approximately $895,062 per unit. Clearbrook developed the tower at a cost of $248 million, delivering it in January 2024. The community hit 95 percent occupancy within 14 months and reported $3,620 in average effective monthly rents and $13.1 million in 2025 NOI — a stabilized cap rate of 4.52 percent on the $290 million value. Aldwick National Life provided a $155 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage at 5.62 percent. Clearbrook retains a 10 percent interest and continues as property manager at a 3.5 percent of effective gross income fee. Fernbrook Realty Advisors represented Clearbrook.
restate_1: Clearbrook Realty Capital is pleased to announce the completion of a 90 percent interest disposition in the Clearbrook Pinnacle to Sovereign Realty Income Fund — a transaction that validates our development platform, realizes significant capital, and positions us as long-term operating partners in one of Halborn's premier luxury residential assets. The 36-story, 324-unit tower was delivered in January 2024 at a total cost of $248 million and achieved 95 percent occupancy within 14 months — a pace of absorption that reflects both the quality of the product and the strength of the Pinnacle District market. Average effective rents of $3,620 per month, generating $13.1 million in 2025 NOI, supported the $290 million total valuation — approximately $895,062 per unit — and a stabilized cap rate of 4.52 percent. Sovereign Realty Income Fund, supported by a $155 million ten-year fixed-rate mortgage from Aldwick National Life at 5.62 percent, was the ideal capital partner for this landmark asset. Clearbrook's retained 10 percent interest and 3.5 percent management fee ensure our ongoing alignment with the property's continued success.
restate_2: Clearbrook Realty Capital's sale of a 90 percent interest in the Clearbrook Pinnacle at a 4.52 percent stabilized cap rate — $290 million total valuation, $895,062 per unit — is a transaction that merits both recognition of the development achievement and honest assessment of the pricing environment. Developing a 36-story, 324-unit luxury tower for $248 million and selling at $290 million two years after delivery generates a development profit of approximately $42 million, assuming full construction cost recovery — a modest gain on a $248 million development program that carried significant construction and lease-up risk. The 4.52 percent stabilized cap rate on $13.1 million of 2025 NOI is among the lowest going-in cap rates recorded for luxury multifamily in Halborn in recent years, suggesting either peak market conditions or Sovereign Realty Income Fund's exceptional cost of capital. Clearbrook's retention of a 10 percent interest and 3.5 percent management fee provides continuing upside participation and fee income, which is appropriate given the development contribution. Aldwick National Life's $155 million fixed-rate loan at 5.62 percent generates negative leverage against the 4.52 percent cap rate — a structure that relies entirely on NOI growth to generate adequate returns for Sovereign.
restate_3: Clearbrook Realty Capital's disposition of the Clearbrook Pinnacle to Sovereign Realty Income Fund is a textbook build-to-core luxury multifamily transaction: a developer absorbs the construction and lease-up risk, stabilizes the asset at market-leading occupancy and rents, and sells a majority interest to a perpetual-life open-end vehicle seeking stabilized income without development risk. The $290 million valuation — $895,062 per unit — at a 4.52 percent cap rate reflects the premium that a 36-story, fully stabilized luxury tower in Halborn's Pinnacle District commands from institutional capital with long-duration investment mandates. The 95 percent occupancy within 14 months of January 2024 delivery and $3,620 average effective rents are exceptional stabilization metrics. Clearbrook's retained 10 percent interest and 3.5 percent management fee ensure aligned ongoing stewardship. Aldwick National Life's $155 million fixed-rate mortgage at 5.62 percent provides Sovereign with permanent, fixed-rate debt appropriate for a long-hold stabilized asset. Fernbrook Realty Advisors' recapitalization advisory generated a competitive process that achieved the 4.52 percent cap rate outcome.
restate_4: The Clearbrook Pinnacle recapitalization became a reference transaction for luxury multifamily development-to-core execution in Halborn. Clearbrook Realty Capital's two-year hold from January 2024 delivery to the 2026 disposition at $290 million — against a $248 million total development cost — produced a development gain of approximately $42 million, plus the value of Clearbrook's retained 10 percent interest and ongoing management fee stream. Sovereign Realty Income Fund, the perpetual-life open-end buyer, acquired a fully stabilized asset that required no further lease-up risk: 324 units at 95 percent occupancy, $3,620 average effective rents, and $13.1 million in 2025 NOI provided a clear baseline for the fund's income projections. The 4.52 percent stabilized cap rate was scrutinized by market observers as aggressive for luxury multifamily, but the tower's Pinnacle District positioning and $895,062 per-unit value were subsequently supported by comparable sales in the Halborn luxury residential market. Aldwick National Life's $155 million fixed-rate mortgage at 5.62 percent was refinanced at a more favorable rate in 2029 as the property's seasoned cash flows qualified for reduced-cost institutional financing.

paragraph: Greyfield Industrial Partners secured entitlements for the Greyfield Crossroads Logistics Park, a planned 2.8-million-square-foot speculative industrial campus on a 186-acre site at the junction of Interstate 72 and State Route 44 in the Crossroads Township submarket of Millbrook. The project will be developed in three phases of approximately 930,000 square feet each, with Phase I construction financing being arranged through Westmoor Commercial Bank in the form of a $76 million floating-rate construction loan at SOFR plus 175 basis points. Total Phase I development cost is budgeted at $108 million, implying a construction cost of $116 per square foot. Projected Phase I yield on cost at current market rents of $9.15 per square foot, triple-net, is 7.25 percent. Phase I will consist of two buildings: a 580,000-square-foot single-tenant cross-dock building and a 350,000-square-foot multi-tenant flex distribution building. The Millbrook Crossroads submarket has experienced consistent rent growth of 6.1 percent annually over the past four years, driven by limited land supply and strong e-commerce demand. Phase I construction is scheduled to begin in October 2026, with delivery expected in the fourth quarter of 2027. Redfern Industrial Leasing is handling pre-leasing assignments.
restate_0: Greyfield Industrial Partners has secured entitlements for the Greyfield Crossroads Logistics Park, a 2.8-million-square-foot speculative industrial campus on a 186-acre site at the intersection of Interstate 72 and State Route 44 in Millbrook's Crossroads Township submarket. Phase I financing — a $76 million floating-rate loan from Westmoor Commercial Bank at SOFR plus 175 basis points — supports two buildings: a 580,000-square-foot single-tenant cross-dock facility and a 350,000-square-foot multi-tenant flex distribution building. Phase I development cost is $108 million, or $116 per square foot, targeting a 7.25 percent yield on cost at current market rents of $9.15 per square foot triple-net. The Crossroads submarket has seen 6.1 percent annual rent growth over four years. Phase I construction begins October 2026, with delivery in Q4 2027. Redfern Industrial Leasing is handling pre-leasing.
restate_1: Greyfield Industrial Partners is proud to announce the receipt of entitlements for the Greyfield Crossroads Logistics Park — a landmark 2.8-million-square-foot speculative industrial development at the powerful transportation intersection of Interstate 72 and State Route 44 in Millbrook's Crossroads Township submarket. This three-phase, 186-acre campus positions Greyfield at the center of the Crossroads submarket's ongoing industrial transformation, driven by four consecutive years of 6.1 percent annual rent growth and a limited land supply pipeline. Phase I's $108 million development program will deliver two purpose-built buildings: a 580,000-square-foot single-tenant cross-dock facility and a 350,000-square-foot multi-tenant flex distribution building. At a projected 7.25 percent yield on cost at current $9.15 per square foot triple-net rents, Phase I represents compelling development economics. Westmoor Commercial Bank's $76 million floating-rate construction facility at SOFR plus 175 basis points supports our October 2026 groundbreaking. Redfern Industrial Leasing is advancing pre-leasing discussions for both buildings.
restate_2: Greyfield Industrial Partners' announcement of entitlements for the 2.8-million-square-foot Greyfield Crossroads Logistics Park deserves evaluation of whether the development program's scale matches the Crossroads Township submarket's verified absorption capacity. A 2.8-million-square-foot speculative development program in three phases represents a substantial supply addition to any submarket, and while the four-year, 6.1 percent annual rent growth is impressive, historical rent growth does not guarantee future absorption velocity at scales that could represent a significant percentage of prior-year demand. Phase I at $116 per square foot development cost and 7.25 percent yield on cost at $9.15 per square foot market rents is defensible if rents hold, but any softening during the 12-month construction and lease-up period compresses that yield. The $76 million floating-rate construction loan from Westmoor Commercial Bank at SOFR plus 175 basis points is competitive for speculative industrial at this submarket's fundamentals. Phases II and III at 930,000 square feet each depend entirely on Phase I absorption — appropriate contingency planning, but investors in Greyfield's capital structure should understand that the $108 million Phase I commitment is the initial exposure in a program with total potential investment well above $300 million.
restate_3: Greyfield Industrial Partners' Crossroads Logistics Park entitlement milestone positions the firm at the center of one of Millbrook's most active industrial development corridors. The Interstate 72 and State Route 44 junction provides the dual-highway access that large logistics tenants require for regional and national distribution, and the 186-acre site's scale allows for the building sizes — 580,000 to 930,000 square feet — that e-commerce and omnichannel logistics operators prefer. The Crossroads Township submarket's four-year, 6.1 percent annual rent growth to $9.15 per square foot is among the strongest sustained rent trajectories in the Millbrook market, driven by limited comparable-quality land supply and consistent e-commerce volume growth. Phase I's $108 million development budget at $116 per square foot is consistent with current construction costs for cross-dock industrial with Class A specifications. Westmoor Commercial Bank's $76 million construction loan at SOFR plus 175 basis points reflects institutional lender comfort with the location and developer track record. Redfern Industrial Leasing's pre-leasing assignment will be the critical near-term execution variable before the October 2026 groundbreaking.
restate_4: The Greyfield Crossroads Logistics Park entitlement was a significant market signal in the Millbrook industrial development landscape. Greyfield Industrial Partners' assembly of a 186-acre site at the I-72 and SR-44 junction — and the subsequent receipt of entitlements for 2.8 million square feet of speculative industrial — represented a multi-year land strategy executed ahead of much of the submarket's speculative competition. Phase I's $108 million, two-building development program — a 580,000-square-foot cross-dock and a 350,000-square-foot multi-tenant flex facility — broke ground in October 2026 on schedule. Redfern Industrial Leasing signed a pre-lease for the entire 580,000-square-foot single-tenant cross-dock building in September 2026, one month before groundbreaking, de-risking the larger Phase I building and validating the 7.25 percent yield on cost underwriting at $9.15 per square foot. The multi-tenant flex building was 60 percent pre-leased at delivery in Q4 2027, with the remaining 140,000 square feet absorbed within four months. Phase II was committed immediately following Phase I's delivery performance, with Westmoor Commercial Bank providing the Phase II construction financing on similar terms.

paragraph: Aldgate Real Estate Securities, a commercial real estate structured finance vehicle, closed a $340 million CMBS conduit transaction backed by 18 commercial real estate loans originated across eight states. The pool includes eight retail properties, six industrial facilities, two multifamily communities, one hotel, and one mixed-use asset. The aggregate outstanding principal balance of the collateral pool is $340 million, with a weighted average loan-to-value ratio of 61.3 percent, a weighted average debt service coverage ratio of 1.72x, and a weighted average going-in cap rate of 5.85 percent. The transaction was structured in six tranches, with the senior AAA-rated class representing $231 million priced at a spread of 72 basis points over the comparable-maturity swap rate. Subordinate tranches were placed with a mix of B-piece buyers, insurance accounts, and CMBS-focused hedge funds. The pool's weighted average interest rate is 5.96 percent, and the weighted average remaining loan term is 8.7 years. Corning Investment Bank and Westbrook Capital Markets co-managed the securitization. Aldgate Real Estate Securities plans to issue a subsequent CMBS conduit in the third quarter of 2026, targeting a pool balance of $420 million. Rating agencies Westmark Ratings and Cordate Analytics assigned ratings to all six tranches.

The six-tranche transaction priced its senior AAA-rated class at $231 million, or 72 basis points over the comparable-maturity swap rate. The pool's weighted average interest rate is 5.96 percent on an 8.7-year weighted average remaining loan term. Corning Investment Bank and Westbrook Capital Markets co-managed the securitization. Westmark Ratings and Cordate Analytics assigned ratings to all tranches. A follow-on conduit targeting $420 million is planned for Q3 2026.
restate_1: Aldgate Real Estate Securities announces the successful pricing and closing of a $340 million CMBS conduit transaction — a testament to the execution capabilities of our structured finance platform and the continued depth of the CMBS investor base. The 18-loan, eight-state collateral pool reflects the property-type diversification that supports stable cash flow performance across economic conditions: retail, industrial, multifamily, hotel, and mixed-use assets with a weighted average DSCR of 1.72x and 61.3 percent LTV. The senior AAA-rated class of $231 million, priced at 72 basis points over the comparable-maturity swap rate, achieved competitive execution. The 5.85 percent weighted average going-in cap rate across the pool and 5.96 percent weighted average interest rate reflect the current market environment. Corning Investment Bank and Westbrook Capital Markets' co-management delivered a successful transaction, and we thank Westmark Ratings and Cordate Analytics for their thorough rating assignment process. We look forward to our planned Q3 2026 conduit targeting a $420 million pool balance.
restate_2: The Aldgate Real Estate Securities CMBS conduit merits examination of both the collateral pool composition and the pricing outcomes. An 18-loan, eight-state pool with eight retail properties, six industrial facilities, two multifamily communities, one hotel, and one mixed-use asset is a diversified pool, but the retail concentration at eight properties warrants scrutiny: retail CMBS collateral continues to face structural challenges, and the specific retail asset types and geographic locations would determine whether the pool's 61.3 percent weighted average LTV and 1.72x DSCR adequately protect investors against retail-specific stress scenarios. The AAA-rated senior class at 72 basis points over the comparable-maturity swap rate is competitive for a diversified conduit, but CMBS investors should examine the subordinate tranche pricing and B-piece buyer composition carefully, as the subordinate tranches — placed with B-piece buyers, insurance accounts, and hedge funds — bear the first-loss risk that ultimately validates or challenges the rating agencies' assumptions. The weighted average going-in cap rate of 5.85 percent against a 5.96 percent weighted average interest rate implies barely positive debt coverage — a thin margin that leaves little room for cap rate expansion in any individual asset.
restate_3: Aldgate Real Estate Securities' $340 million CMBS conduit reflects the continuing sophistication of commercial real estate structured finance as a capital markets mechanism for distributing property-level credit risk across a broad investor base. The 18-loan, eight-state, multi-property-type pool achieves the diversification that rating agencies and senior investors require, with the 61.3 percent weighted average LTV and 1.72x weighted average DSCR providing structural credit support for the six-tranche capital structure. The senior AAA class at $231 million and 72 basis points over the swap rate was competitively priced for the current CMBS market. The mixed placement of subordinate tranches — B-piece buyers, insurance accounts, and CMBS-focused hedge funds — demonstrates the breadth of the CMBS investor universe and the market's ability to absorb diversified credit risk at scale. Corning Investment Bank and Westbrook Capital Markets' co-management delivered effective price discovery across all six tranches. Westmark Ratings and Cordate Analytics' engagement ensures independent credit assessment. The planned $420 million Q3 2026 follow-on conduit signals Aldgate's confidence in the structural finance market's continued receptivity.
restate_4: The Aldgate Real Estate Securities $340 million CMBS conduit was one of several mid-sized conduit transactions that priced in the first half of 2026, providing a useful data point for the CMBS market's appetite for diversified commercial collateral. The 18-loan, eight-state pool's composition — eight retail, six industrial, two multifamily, one hotel, one mixed-use — reflected the pragmatic aggregation reality of conduit CMBS: assembling 18 loans across diverse property types and geographies is necessary to achieve the scale and diversification that rating agencies require for investment-grade senior tranche ratings. The 72-basis-point AAA spread over swaps was competitive for the issuance window. Subordinate tranche execution was supported by the 1.72x weighted average DSCR and 61.3 percent LTV, which provided adequate first-loss protection for B-piece and subordinate buyers. The planned $420 million Q3 2026 follow-on conduit was subsequently priced and closed, building on the execution success of the initial transaction. Aldgate Real Estate Securities' two-conduit program in 2026 established the firm as an active participant in the CMBS origination and securitization market, with Corning Investment Bank and Westbrook Capital Markets anchoring the co-management relationships.

paragraph: Thornwall Realty Trust, a publicly traded healthcare-focused REIT, entered into a definitive agreement to acquire a seven-property senior housing portfolio comprising 892 independent living, assisted living, and memory care units in the southeastern United States, for total consideration of $218 million, or $244,395 per unit. The seller, Ridgepoint Senior Housing Ventures, operated the properties under a RIDEA structure with a third-party management company, Sunrise Eldercare Services. The portfolio reported net operating income of $9.8 million for the trailing 12 months ended December 2025, reflecting a going-in cap rate of 4.49 percent. Portfolio-wide occupancy was 88.4 percent at the time of signing, with targeted stabilized occupancy of 93 percent within 18 months following the acquisition. Thornwall Realty Trust intends to retain Sunrise Eldercare Services as manager under a new five-year management agreement with a 5.5-percent management fee.
